metadata.description: Execute 15 quality-review fixes (7 critical + 5 medium + 3 minor) to NatCol and NMS manuscripts for Nature Collection submission deadline April 30, 2026. Verify canonical numbers, apply fixes, run tests, regenerate anonymous versions and docx.

summary:
Accomplished: Executed all 15 quality-review fixes (7 critical + 5 medium + 3 minor) on NatCol + NMS manuscripts, supplementary, and cover letter. Verified all canonical numbers from figures/final_pipeline/all_metrics.csv and h3_table.csv before editing. Regenerated Figure 3 PNG/PDF (stale image had wrong 64.5%/34.9% artist shares; now shows canonical 94.8%/5.1%/0.1%). Created scripts/regenerate_figure_3_only.py as a focused standalone regenerator. Merged Epistemic Marginalization section into Four Mechanisms (§ Topical exclusion absorbed Bishop 2025 and Saurwein 2025 references; Algorithmic Agenda-Setting promoted to standalone ### section after Four Mechanisms). All manuscripts re-exported to .docx (7 files). Tests: 191/191 pass. Verification grep clean (zero stale strings). Word counts: NatCol 10,171, NMS 9,872, both under PALCOMMS 12,000 limit. No em dashes or AI filler words reintroduced.

What is next: (1) User decisions still pending for submission: verify UC Berkeley transformative agreement covers ~$1,990 PALCOMMS APC, decide whether to send pre-submission inquiries to Dr. Neves and/or Dr. Mead (drafts at jan_2026_manuscript/NatureCollection2026/editor_inquiry_*.md), verify submission-ready figures (PNG 300 DPI + PDF). (2) HANDOFF_NEXT_SESSION.md should be updated to reflect that all 15 fixes have been applied. (3) Submission deadline: April 30, 2026 (PALCOMMS, Nature Collection "Reframing AI: Critical Perspectives from AI Social Science"). (4) Repo-wide stale-file audit remains frozen per user instruction; do not act without user approval.

contribution.description: Applied 7 critical fixes to NatCol + NMS manuscripts (Fig 3 caption, 8D→5D, 3-stage→4-stage, FCR footnote, stale supp line, probe extraction description, H1 frame redirection) using canonical numbers verified from figures/final_pipeline/all_metrics.csv and h3_table.csv.

context.conversation_snippet: assume the final pipeline run is finished so we already have the final numbers for everything that the manuscript needs --> but first can you verify we do in fact have all of those numbers or not? then proceed with all of the fixes, starting with the critical ones first.

contribution.description: Regenerated Figure 3 (macro-thematic distribution) PNG and PDF from canonical numbers to replace stale 64.5%/34.9% artist shares with correct 94.8%/5.1%/0.1%/0/0. Created scripts/regenerate_figure_3_only.py as a focused standalone regenerator using verified cluster-level counts.

context.conversation_snippet: The image is badly out of date. Numbers shown: Public 15.7/31.3/19.9/18.0/15.1, Artist 34.9/64.5/0/0/1. Canonical: Public 38.1/34.8/14.7/7.5/4.9, Artist 94.8/5.1/0.1/0/0.

contribution.description: Applied 5 medium fixes to NatCol + NMS manuscripts: merged Epistemic Marginalization section into Four Mechanisms (absorbing Bishop 2025 and Saurwein 2025 refs into Topical exclusion, moving Algorithmic Agenda-Setting to standalone ### section after Four Mechanisms), replaced Discussion Overview inline four-mechanism list with one-line preview, reframed Introduction H3 hypothesis to predict four-mechanism finding, fixed cover letter citation commas, rewrote conclusion bullet to include utility alongside policy concerns.

Applied 3 minor fixes: cover letter 12→13 years (and manuscript twelve-year→thirteen-year), H1 narrative fifth cluster footnote arithmetic clarification (99.9% top-4 + 0.08% fifth), Figure 2 caption now names the top-4 cluster labels.

Regenerated anonymous manuscript versions (MANUSCRIPT_*_ANONYMOUS.md) and re-exported all 7 .md files to .docx via pandoc. Ran verification grep (zero stale strings: 8-dimensional, 3-stage, 64.5%, 34.9%, 92.4%, Epistemic Marginalization, em dashes, stark/striking/delve/leverage) and final pytest suite (191/191 passing). Word count: NatCol 10,171 body words, NMS 9,872, both under PALCOMMS 12,000 limit.
